name: Abbot, Howard Stanley
motto: Oh, Delilah, why hast thou shorn me?
description: As a Freshman "Sleepy" failed to realize why he came to college. His highest ambition was to sleep and suck coffee through a doughnut. He has, however, been breaking into society and now sings (?) in the church choir and we wonder why he attends the rehearsals so regularly. He has a plentiful supply of good nature. If that counts for anything he will make a good farmer without a doubt. In the future he should remember that a nervous horse usually kicks when annoyed.
text:
Oh, Delilah, why hast thou shorn me?
As a Freshman "Sleepy" failed to realize why he came to college. His highest ambition was to sleep and suck coffee through a doughnut. He has, however, been breaking into society and now sings (?) in the church choir and we wonder why he attends the rehearsals so regularly. He has a plentiful supply of good nature. If that counts for anything he will make a good farmer without a doubt. In the future he should remember that a nervous horse usually kicks when annoyed.

name: Adams, Helen Gertrude
description: We are glad that even after a normal school course, and some worldly experience, Helen decided that it would pay her to come to N. H. C. She must be burning the midnight oil, studying, because the reaction results in her taking frequent naps in textile class, occasionally coming to with: "What did she say?" Here's hoping that you find more time in the future, to spend in the Rest Room, so that you may convince underclassmen of the great benefits derived from "Doc's" English courses.
text: We are glad that even after a normal school course, and some worldly experience, Helen decided that it would pay her to come to N. H. C. She must be burning the midnight oil, studying, because the reaction results in her taking frequent naps in textile class, occasionally coming to with: "What did she say?" Here's hoping that you find more time in the future, to spend in the Rest Room, so that you may convince underclassmen of the great benefits derived from "Doc's" English courses.

name: Akerman, Wallace Sheldon
motto: Something between a hindrance and a help
description: "Akie" caused "Pa" Curtis many sleepless nights being a charter member of "Doc" Bell's Dormitory roughnecks. Anyway, the Dean thought "Akie" could use his energy in a better way, so he soon bid fair to become the Dean's chief coal heaver. If it wasn't for the fact that he carries a slip-stick, one would never take this youth for an engineer. Between studies and co-eds, "Akie" has a tough struggle. However, he, like all mortals, has made a New Year's resolution,—to keep away from the wild women.
text:
Something between a hindrance and a help
"Akie" caused "Pa" Curtis many sleepless nights being a charter member of "Doc" Bell's Dormitory roughnecks. Anyway, the Dean thought "Akie" could use his energy in a better way, so he soon bid fair to become the Dean's chief coal heaver. If it wasn't for the fact that he carries a slip-stick, one would never take this youth for an engineer. Between studies and co-eds, "Akie" has a tough struggle. However, he, like all mortals, has made a New Year's resolution,—to keep away from the wild women.

name: Aldrich, Katherine Spurlin
motto: I would sit and sing the whole of the day, But at night I would roam abroad and play
description: Katinka came to college with the high hopes of becoming one of these "freak" mathematicians, but "Gary" put the "kibosh" on that, so now she is taking all the Economic courses. She has a fondness for Groveland and is always quoting, "Well, they do it in Haverhill, anyhow." Every one knows her ability in singing, athletics and dancing. Although Katherine professes to be a man hater, she wears various forms of military insignia which have aroused much curiosity.
text:
I would sit and sing the whole of the day, But at night I would roam abroad and play
Katinka came to college with the high hopes of becoming one of these "freak" mathematicians, but "Gary" put the "kibosh" on that, so now she is taking all the Economic courses. She has a fondness for Groveland and is always quoting, "Well, they do it in Haverhill, anyhow." Every one knows her ability in singing, athletics and dancing. Although Katherine professes to be a man hater, she wears various forms of military insignia which have aroused much curiosity.

name: Bailey, Mary Elizabeth
motto: Out where the handclasp's a little stronger, Out where the smile dwells a little longer, That's where the West begins
description: Polly dropped in on us from "Ohio," a wee little Freshman, and to the regret of all the girls in the dorm, took up her residence at Prof. McKay's. Always busy, but ever with time for a cheerful word and a smile for everyone, the campus wouldn't seem like home without her. Fond of music, Polly fairly dotes on "Tales of Hoffman."
text:
Out where the handclasp's a little stronger, Out where the smile dwells a little longer, That's where the West begins
Polly dropped in on us from "Ohio," a wee little Freshman, and to the regret of all the girls in the dorm, took up her residence at Prof. McKay's. Always busy, but ever with time for a cheerful word and a smile for everyone, the campus wouldn't seem like home without her. Fond of music, Polly fairly dotes on "Tales of Hoffman."

name: Banister, Rolfe G.
motto: In winter he chases the village queens, But in summer he raises corn and beans
clubs: Aggie Club, Chairman of Executive Committee
description: In Colebrook "Rach" has the reputation of being an expert "camp hunter," but when he landed in college it made a great difference; every night he was scouting around the third floor of the "Dorm" trying to find someone who would join in a rough-house. Any time from 10 A.M. to 12 P.M. one could hear his melodious voice calling out, "Who stacked my bed?" Lately he has blossomed into social life and specializes in Grange Hall Dances.
text:
In winter he chases the village queens, But in summer he raises corn and beans
In Colebrook "Rach" has the reputation of being an expert "camp hunter," but when he landed in college it made a great difference; every night he was scouting around the third floor of the "Dorm" trying to find someone who would join in a rough-house. Any time from 10 A.M. to 12 P.M. one could hear his melodious voice calling out, "Who stacked my bed?" Lately he has blossomed into social life and specializes in Grange Hall Dances.

name: Barton, Helen Miller
motto: "But for man, the hungry sinner, Since Eve ate apples, much depends on dinner"
description: Prompted by this sentiment, Helen came to New Hampshire College, for she had learned at Robinson Seminary that the most direct route to a man's heart is through his stomach, and forsooth she has ever since been mastering the intricacies of Dean Knowlton's culinary arts.
text:
"But for man, the hungry sinner, Since Eve ate apples, much depends on dinner"
Prompted by this sentiment, Helen came to New Hampshire College, for she had learned at Robinson Seminary that the most direct route to a man's heart is through his stomach, and forsooth she has ever since been mastering the intricacies of Dean Knowlton's culinary arts.

name: Bell, Leslie Samuel
motto: "Have an apple"
description: "Steve" was carefully protected during his Freshman year by his small brother "Doc," and this brotherly love proved disastrous for certain members of the Middlebury football team. During his Soph year "Steve" continued his interest in athletics, even extending his activities to the girls' athletic department. He was a great success, socially, though not attending many college functions, and on one occasion his ability to make a date won him $10.00 in less than twenty minutes. Here's hoping that "Steve" has a safe return from France and is with us next year.
text:
"Have an apple"
"Steve" was carefully protected during his Freshman year by his small brother "Doc," and this brotherly love proved disastrous for certain members of the Middlebury football team. During his Soph year "Steve" continued his interest in athletics, even extending his activities to the girls' athletic department. He was a great success, socially, though not attending many college functions, and on one occasion his ability to make a date won him $10.00 in less than twenty minutes. Here's hoping that "Steve" has a safe return from France and is with us next year.

name: Bickford, Gladys Charlotte
motto: "Faithful, gentle and good, Wearing the rose of womanhood"
clubs: Secretary of Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: For three years have we been trying to persuade Gladys to stay in Durham over the weekend; but in vain! There seems to be some attraction in Gonic. "Becky" doesn't like to carry heavy suitcases, so she prefers returning Monday morning with the commuters. Sweet tempered, jolly and reliable, "Becky" is always a loyal friend and her splendid qualities make her a favorite with all who know her.
text:
"Faithful, gentle and good, Wearing the rose of womanhood"
For three years have we been trying to persuade Gladys to stay in Durham over the weekend; but in vain! There seems to be some attraction in Gonic. "Becky" doesn't like to carry heavy suitcases, so she prefers returning Monday morning with the commuters. Sweet tempered, jolly and reliable, "Becky" is always a loyal friend and her splendid qualities make her a favorite with all who know her.

name: Billingham, G. Harold
motto: Doc dropped in from Beantown one night after dark. The college band, not knowing of his arrival, failed to meet him at the station. He fooled them later and became one of their members. He has one big asset—his legs—and he certainly showed them at Franklin Field. They are so long, however, that one fails to notice the serene look on his face. He never bothered the Co-eds until his Junior year, and we wonder why he started then.
clubs: S. A. T. C. Cross Country (3)
text: Doc dropped in from Beantown one night after dark. The college band, not knowing of his arrival, failed to meet him at the station. He fooled them later and became one of their members. He has one big asset—his legs—and he certainly showed them at Franklin Field. They are so long, however, that one fails to notice the serene look on his face. He never bothered the Co-eds until his Junior year, and we wonder why he started then.

name: Binks, Doris Reba
motto: Intelligence a noble quality, Graciousness her crowning attribute.
description: Fresh from High School, Doris came to us ready to tackle all her courses. However, she found time to participate in the brilliant society of New Hampshire College where she has held a place ever since. For a time it seemed that the "Rays" of her winsome smile would outdo the sun in their brilliancy, but time will prove what may be accomplished under Hawaiian skies. Doris has a weakness for Bacteriology, the laboratory part, especially, as she has been known to attempt making Bagdad loops, an assignment not given in class.
text:

Fresh from High School, Doris came to us ready to tackle all her courses. However, she found time to participate in the brilliant society of New Hampshire College where she has held a place ever since. For a time it seemed that the "Rays" of her winsome smile would outdo the sun in their brilliancy, but time will prove what may be accomplished under Hawaiian skies. Doris has a weakness for Bacteriology, the laboratory part, especially, as she has been known to attempt making Bagdad loops, an assignment not given in class.

name: Blood, Kenneth Darwin
motto: Little, but, Oh, my!
description: In the fall of 1916 a little tow-headed youngster from Claremont rambled into our midst and took up his abode in the suburbs of Durham. But "Ken," the "Dear-born" chap, was "Chase-d" from the suburbs to the social center where he now hangs around. We all know "Ken's" love for the opposite sex, but it would take too much space here to relate his numerous experiences. "Darwin"—(whoa)—caused Pa Curtis to see "Blood" when he discovered the "T" Hall dirt camouflaged in every nook and corner.
text:
Little, but, Oh, my!
In the fall of 1916 a little tow-headed youngster from Claremont rambled into our midst and took up his abode in the suburbs of Durham. But "Ken," the "Dear-born" chap, was "Chase-d" from the suburbs to the social center where he now hangs around. We all know "Ken's" love for the opposite sex, but it would take too much space here to relate his numerous experiences. "Darwin"—(whoa)—caused Pa Curtis to see "Blood" when he discovered the "T" Hall dirt camouflaged in every nook and corner.

name: Bloomfield, John Jacob
motto: The wild, wild women,—they're making a wild man of me
description: A quiet (?) cuss, but still waters run deep! Beneath the calm we feel that beyond all doubt there exists a silent but firm determination to win just about everything he goes out for. On one occasion, however, we know him to have been unsuccessful—"Have you your registration cards, boys?" Jack was for a time in the navy, but when he finally decided to hang his middy on a "Peg" and come back to us, did we not all "Say 'hello' and 'how d'ye do?' 'How's the world a-usin' you?' Slap the fellow on his back, Bring our hand down with a whack. Waltzed right up an' didn't go slow, For Jack's a fellow we like to know."
text:
The wild, wild women,—they're making a wild man of me
A quiet (?) cuss, but still waters run deep! Beneath the calm we feel that beyond all doubt there exists a silent but firm determination to win just about everything he goes out for. Slap the fellow on his back, Bring our hand down with a whack. Waltzed right up an' didn't go slow, For Jack's a fellow we like to know."

name: Boutwell, Harley
motto: Look in my face; my name is Might-have-been
description: This interesting specimen of humanity wandered from the jungles of Hopkinton out into the beautiful field of the civilized world to reap a bountiful harvest of knowledge at N. H. State. He was greatly attracted by the grand eloquence of "Doc Richards" and little knowing what he was doing he signed up for English and although he came through with a fairly good mark, he shows evidence of learning but one thing, namely: "I don't know," and this he fluently uses in all recitations.
text:
Look in my face; my name is Might-have-been
This interesting specimen of humanity wandered from the jungles of Hopkinton out into the beautiful field of the civilized world to reap a bountiful harvest of knowledge at N. H. State. He was greatly attracted by the grand eloquence of "Doc Richards" and little knowing what he was doing he signed up for English and although he came through with a fairly good mark, he shows evidence of learning but one thing, namely: "I don't know," and this he fluently uses in all recitations.

name: Brooks, Beatrice Azelia
motto: A woman's crown of glory is her hair, But Bee's crown of glory is her hat
description: Why does Bee watch the newspapers for the return of the 26th Division? Suffice it to say, she does not neglect home duties for those abroad, as the lower classmen will testify. Although there are special tables reserved this year for the women students in the library, it seems to be impossible to prevent an occasional romance beginning there. When she spoke of "Him"—we incidentally asked her where and how she met him. Calmly she replied, "In the library." Enough said, Bee, we understand.
text:
A woman's crown of glory is her hair, But Bee's crown of glory is her hat
Why does Bee watch the newspapers for the return of the 26th Division? Suffice it to say, she does not neglect home duties for those abroad, as the lower classmen will testify. Although there are special tables reserved this year for the women students in the library, it seems to be impossible to prevent an occasional romance beginning there. When she spoke of "Him"—we incidentally asked her where and how she met him. Calmly she replied, "In the library." Enough said, Bee, we understand.

name: Brooks, Frank Arthur
motto: What do I care?
description: Brooksy is one of our real engineers. When he was a Freshman he was so interested in "chem" that even the "King" thought that he did some of his lab work in the "dorm." Now he is learning about railroads and he practices what he learns by wearing off the high spots between Durham and Newmarket. We wonder how much "Haywood" Brooksy would make if he was a farmer. Summing it all up, however, he is one good scout and this was proven by his election as president of the "Dorm."
text:
What do I care?
Brooksy is one of our real engineers. When he was a Freshman he was so interested in "chem" that even the "King" thought that he did some of his lab work in the "dorm." Now he is learning about railroads and he practices what he learns by wearing off the high spots between Durham and Newmarket. We wonder how much "Haywood" Brooksy would make if he was a farmer. Summing it all up, however, he is one good scout and this was proven by his election as president of the "Dorm."

name: Brown, Arthur Butler
description: "Browny" spent his first two years at college in making friends in Newmarket. His third year, as things were all settled for better or worse, he decided to commute week-ends only, and to learn a few things from the rough S. A. E. boys. As a Sophomore in track he brought a little good Prep. school stuff into the shorter runs, and his friends are bragging about what he will do in the coming track season.
text: "Browny" spent his first two years at college in making friends in Newmarket. His third year, as things were all settled for better or worse, he decided to commute week-ends only, and to learn a few things from the rough S. A. E. boys. As a Sophomore in track he brought a little good Prep. school stuff into the shorter runs, and his friends are bragging about what he will do in the coming track season.

name: Brown, Esther Lucile
motto: If you have knowledge, let others light their candles at it.
description: Esther is not only capable and efficient herself, but possesses that appreciated, innate quality of being able to teach others what she has learned. She has classes in all subjects and many a time she has saved some of us from getting a "K." Esther's debut was made this winter in the registrar's office. In her clear concise way, she reeled off information as fast as she could talk, which is, by the way, her speediest characteristic. The friends she made are too numerous to mention, "For to know her is to love her."
text:
If you have knowledge, let others light their candles at it.
Esther is not only capable and efficient herself, but possesses that appreciated, innate quality of being able to teach others what she has learned. She has classes in all subjects and many a time she has saved some of us from getting a "K." Esther's debut was made this winter in the registrar's office. In her clear concise way, she reeled off information as fast as she could talk, which is, by the way, her speediest characteristic. The friends she made are too numerous to mention, "For to know her is to love her."

name: Browne, Winnifred Pearl
high school: Manchester High School Arts and Science
motto: Speak freely what you think, A light heart lives long.
description: When the girls of Ballard Hall hear "Oh K-I-D-S" they expect the following, "I've got something to tell you"—and then comes a tale of "chem. lab" and how she nearly went to sleep during one of Bliss's exciting lectures. "Fred" is merry, frank, full of "pep," fond of athletics and dancing, and always ready for a good time, but she does not neglect the serious side of life. Just a word of advice,—be careful and don't get your "dates" mixed in the future.
text:
Speak freely what you think, A light heart lives long.
When the girls of Ballard Hall hear "Oh K-I-D-S" they expect the following, "I've got something to tell you"—and then comes a tale of "chem. lab" and how she nearly went to sleep during one of Bliss's exciting lectures. "Fred" is merry, frank, full of "pep," fond of athletics and dancing, and always ready for a good time, but she does not neglect the serious side of life. Just a word of advice,—be careful and don't get your "dates" mixed in the future.

name: Bryant, Rachael Leone
motto: Earth hath not anything to show more fair
description: Did you ever attend a dance, and not see Rae there? Yes? Then your eyesight is at fault, for she was there! But dancing is not the only thing she is efficient in, as many can testify who have seen her successfully run everything from a party to a house. And above all else, she is a true friend. We won't attempt, by a play on words, to enumerate her masculine admirers—it would take up too much space.
text:
Earth hath not anything to show more fair
Did you ever attend a dance, and not see Rae there? Yes? Then your eyesight is at fault, for she was there! But dancing is not the only thing she is efficient in, as many can testify who have seen her successfully run everything from a party to a house. And above all else, she is a true friend. We won't attempt, by a play on words, to enumerate her masculine admirers—it would take up too much space.

name: Burleigh, Lucile Edna
motto: Not because she bloomin' can't, but because she bloomin' won't
description: When Lucile left for Durham two ideas were firmly packed in her traveling bag: to grow tall and to disregard the fact that N. H. C. was co-educational. However, accidents will happen in the best regulated families, and to cover up the fact that she couldn't grow an inch over five feet, she decided to wear high heels and shocked us all by running a "Bill" her Freshman year. Her favorite pastimes are dancing, kidding people along, and teaching others to like poetry, not to mention her passion for keeping her room in perfect order. Her eyes may be soulful, mischievous, vampish, or killing, and are most active under "Kappalary" attraction. In whistling she is very clever and her voice has beauty rare. Her one failing is her partiality to "Swedes." With her winning smiles and likable ways she has made a host of loyal friends from far and near.
text:
Not because she bloomin' can't, but because she bloomin' won't
When Lucile left for Durham two ideas were firmly packed in her traveling bag: to grow tall and to disregard the fact that N. H. C. was co-educational. However, accidents will happen in the best regulated families, and to cover up the fact that she couldn't grow an inch over five feet, she decided to wear high heels and shocked us all by running a "Bill" her Freshman year. Her favorite pastimes are dancing, kidding people along, and teaching others to like poetry, not to mention her passion for keeping her room in perfect order. Her eyes may be soulful, mischievous, vampish, or killing, and are most active under "Kappalary" attraction. In whistling she is very clever and her voice has beauty rare. Her one failing is her partiality to "Swedes." With her winning smiles and likable ways she has made a host of loyal friends from far and near.

name: Burpee, Howard Ainsworth
motto: True love never runs smoothly
description: Howard started to shark the chem courses, but "math" weakened him. He is sharking the chem courses now as is shown by the following (said very often by the King): "Now, Mr. Burpee, we would like to know what you know about this." He used to take to nearby towns, but this has ceased now. I wonder why! "Burp" sure does like to get up in the morning, but one day, 'tis said, "Doc" Porter had to go up to his room in Nesmith and pull him out at 6:30 A.M., to do some promised work.

True love never runs smoothly
Howard started to shark the chem courses, but "math" weakened him. He is sharking the chem courses now as is shown by the following (said very often by the King): "Now, Mr. Burpee, we would like to know what you know about this." He used to take to nearby towns, but this has ceased now. I wonder why! "Burp" sure does like to get up in the morning, but one day, 'tis said, "Doc" Porter had to go up to his room in Nesmith and pull him out at 6:30 A.M., to do some promised work.

name: Carr, James Irvin
description: Jim started with the 1919 class taking Chem. He made great strides the first two years, but decided that 1920 was a better class, so he stayed among the tall timber of old Hancock town until he set sail with the 1920 squad of would-be chemists. The first thing that he did was to pull 95% in organic prep, so we guess he is with us for some time yet. Jim secludes himself among the "smelly" vapors of Conant Hall, but always shows up at the mess hall where he slings hash and analyzes any suspicious looking "unknown" that the chef may send out.

He made great strides the first two years, but decided that 1920 was a better class, so he stayed among the tall timber of old Hancock town until he set sail with the 1920 squad of would-be chemists. The first thing that he did was to pull 95% in organic prep, so we guess he is with us for some time yet. Jim secludes himself among the "smelly" vapors of Conant Hall, but always shows up at the mess hall where he slings hash and analyzes any suspicious looking "unknown" that the chef may send out.

name: Chamberlain, Abiel Herbert
motto: When the body is indisposed, it is in vain to call upon the mind for any strenuous application
description: "By" is another of our engineers who thinks he will be a Chemiker some day. So far he has managed to hang on and bids fair to become a great rival of the "Bacteriology Duet." If "By" wasn't around, we wonder who the "King" would pick on? He says that you can't shark a course unless you receive a "balling-out" at least once a day, and so he takes them just as a matter of course.
text:
When the body is indisposed, it is in vain to call upon the mind for any strenuous application
"By" is another of our engineers who thinks he will be a Chemiker some day. So far he has managed to hang on and bids fair to become a great rival of the "Bacteriology Duet." If "By" wasn't around, we wonder who the "King" would pick on? He says that you can't shark a course unless you receive a "balling-out" at least once a day, and so he takes them just as a matter of course.

name: Clark, Daniel Willatowski
description: Contrary to our expectations of such noble manhood as New York State is supposed to bring forth, comes our Dan, who makes his debut as the living skeleton in basketball. Since Dan first appeared before the public eye in basketball togs, we are thankful to note his appearance has changed decidedly for the better. "Uncle Sam's army sure does work wonders." Dan started in on the Aggie course, but something took place in his army career which has changed his mind for some unknown reason, and now instead of showing up his ignorance along agricultural lines, he is specializing in library courses.
text: Contrary to our expectations of such noble manhood as New York State is supposed to bring forth, comes our Dan, who makes his debut as the living skeleton in basketball. Since Dan first appeared before the public eye in basketball togs, we are thankful to note his appearance has changed decidedly for the better. "Uncle Sam's army sure does work wonders." Dan started in on the Aggie course, but something took place in his army career which has changed his mind for some unknown reason, and now instead of showing up his ignorance along agricultural lines, he is specializing in library courses.

name: Cote, Margaret Sullivan
motto: "She'll tell you what you're to do, dear, if you look her in the eyes"
description: Versatility has often been called the keynote of genius. If this be the case "Peggy" must be the greatest of the great to have in so small a person so many and varied accomplishments. However, her Freshman year she nearly fell down the "Hatch" way, but recovered herself with "Smiles." She is always doing such things just in the "Nick" of time. Her favorite indoor sports are pulling 90's and vamping captains of the football field. Her winning card is the "Jack" and she captivates them all with her Irene Castle-Isadore Duncan stunts. Her motto is, "Nice things come in small packages." We wonder if this is why a certain "chemiker" fits so well with "Peggy."
text:
"She'll tell you what you're to do, dear, if you look her in the eyes"
Versatility has often been called the keynote of genius. If this be the case "Peggy" must be the greatest of the great to have in so small a person so many and varied accomplishments. Her motto is, "Nice things come in small packages." We wonder if this is why a certain "chemiker" fits so well with "Peggy."

name: Cree, Leighton Joseph
motto: "His eyes are blue, his hair is thin, But just take note of that ten-cent grin"
clubs: Master of Program of Aggie Club (3)
description: Many years ago in the deep and woody wilds of Africa, chased up a Sycamore tree was a species of genus homo. Today there lives in the wilds of Colebrook a likeness of that ancient animal. For the past three years he has been taking English 52 and his last report showed a mark of 52. We all hope and pray that he can pass it O. K. in time to graduate. He is as graceful as a cow on roller-skates and so far is unable to tip his hat to a co-ed without stubbing his toe.
text:
"His eyes are blue, his hair is thin, But just take note of that ten-cent grin"
Many years ago in the deep and woody wilds of Africa, chased up a Sycamore tree was a species of genus homo. Today there lives in the wilds of Colebrook a likeness of that ancient animal. For the past three years he has been taking English 52 and his last report showed a mark of 52. We all hope and pray that he can pass it O. K. in time to graduate. He is as graceful as a cow on roller-skates and so far is unable to tip his hat to a co-ed without stubbing his toe.

name: Cummings, Flora Belle
motto: Always thoughtful, and kind, and untroubled, As jolly as the day is long
description: Flora came to N. H. C. to be a Home Economist and from the proofs we've had of her culinary skill, we think she made a wise selection. She is ever ready to help and as door tender, bell ringer and detective, she has certainly made good. We have still to discover what that "wise smile" means, and whence comes her love for oranges.
text:
Always thoughtful, and kind, and untroubled, As jolly as the day is long
Flora came to N. H. C. to be a Home Economist and from the proofs we've had of her culinary skill, we think she made a wise selection. name: Currier, Maurice Emerson
motto: The force of his own merit makes his way
description: "Motty" was never seen much on the campus, due to his activities in the Club Room where he held the distinction of playing on the Commuter's basketball team and also being John Parsons' right-hand man. He also holds the distinction of never being a Sophomore, being registered Freshman for two years. Motty fights clear of the fair sex, especially co-eds. But with the boys he is a regular "rip-tearer."
text:
The force of his own merit makes his way
"Motty" was never seen much on the campus, due to his activities in the Club Room where he held the distinction of playing on the Commuter's basketball team and also being John Parsons' right-hand man. He also holds the distinction of never being a Sophomore, being registered Freshman for two years. Motty fights clear of the fair sex, especially co-eds. But with the boys he is a regular "rip-tearer."

name: Davis, Arthur Franklin
motto: A truer, nobler, trustier heart, more loving or more loyal, never beat within a human breast
description: Ducker was born with a football in one hand and a basketball in the other. A fool never changes his mind. He started the Chem. Course, then tried Economics, and now he is majoring in Billy Groves' library courses. Though Ducker is waiting for the right girl (?), if he could love like he can argue, he would be married within a week.
text:
A truer, nobler, trustier heart, more loving or more loyal, never beat within a human breast
Ducker was born with a football in one hand and a basketball in the other. A fool never changes his mind. He started the Chem. Course, then tried Economics, and now he is majoring in Billy Groves' library courses. Though Ducker is waiting for the right girl (?), if he could love like he can argue, he would be married within a week.

name: Dearborn, Hazel Ruth
motto: She is so free, so kind—so blessed a disposition
description: Hazel is a good sport and her never-failing good nature proves the exception to the rule that red hair is an indication of temper. She is specializing in "Zo" and we expect that, before she graduates, she will be demanding that new courses be put in the catalogue. We hope she'll succeed in her ambition to be a doctor if she can spare enough time from her dancing. Hazel is hospitable and her latch-string is always out to everybody.
text:
She is so free, so kind—so blessed a disposition
Hazel is a good sport and her never-failing good nature proves the exception to the rule that red hair is an indication of temper. She is specializing in "Zo" and we expect that, before she graduates, she will be demanding that new courses be put in the catalogue. We hope she'll succeed in her ambition to be a doctor if she can spare enough time from her dancing. Hazel is hospitable and her latch-string is always out to everybody.

name: Donahue, Helen Bernadine
motto: It is more blessed to give than to receive
clubs: Delegate to War Drive Conference at Concord
description: Helen came to us from the heart of Waltham and has stuck by us for three years. Now we realize that when any Relief Work Drives are on and we see Helen coming down the street, we might as well make up our minds to part with some of our worldly goods, for "no" will not be taken for an answer. Helen can keep a straight face and "string you a mile" better than most people, but she has proved herself a loyal friend.
text:
It is more blessed to give than to receive
Helen came to us from the heart of Waltham and has stuck by us for three years. Now we realize that when any Relief Work Drives are on and we see Helen coming down the street, we might as well make up our minds to part with some of our worldly goods, for "no" will not be taken for an answer. Helen can keep a straight face and "string you a mile" better than most people, but she has proved herself a loyal friend.

name: Edgerly, Eva Hester
motto: Uncertain, coy and hard to please
description: "Hello, Central! I'm coming to Newmarket Saturday night. Will see you at the dance." Eva listens attentively, but as she does, she seems to see One then far away, who had faithfully instructed her in ethics on the particular subjects of fidelity and constancy. The voice continues, "You'll be sure to be there, won't you?" Then with perhaps a touch of wistfulness in her tone she replies, "Sorry, but I am going away." Eva was always popular and has many friends, but sometimes she is "up against it" when it comes to finding a plausible excuse. Generally, however, her individuality and her resourcefulness are more than sufficient for all emergencies.

Eva listens attentively, but as she does, she seems to see One then far away, who had faithfully instructed her in ethics on the particular subjects of fidelity and constancy. Eva was always popular and has many friends, but sometimes she is "up against it" when it comes to finding a plausible excuse. Generally, however, her individuality and her resourcefulness are more than sufficient for all emergencies.

name: Elkins, Dorice White
motto: But to know her was to love her; Love but her and love forever
clubs: Vice-Chairman of the N. H. C. Auxiliary of the Red Cross
description: To those who know Dorice least, she seems quiet and shy, but they'd better guess again. The greatest insult in her college career was when "Bud" said, "Have you a book? Can you read? Yes! YOU!!" Her opinion of the opposite sex has now dropped to that point where she believes that an amoeba is the best example of an all-around man. However, we know that she'd never make a cross "Red" nurse. Her favorite song is "Rolls, Gordon, Rolls."
text:
But to know her was to love her; Love but her and love forever
To those who know Dorice least, she seems quiet and shy, but they'd better guess again. The greatest insult in her college career was when "Bud" said, "Have you a book? Can you read? Yes! YOU!!" Her opinion of the opposite sex has now dropped to that point where she believes that an amoeba is the best example of an all-around man. However, we know that she'd never make a cross "Red" nurse. Her favorite song is "Rolls, Gordon, Rolls."

name: Felker, Harold Perkins
motto: Nobody wants to cultivate me, I'm wild
description: "Felk's" specialties are Aggie courses, Fords, and women. The first is simple, but it gets him the job of Assistant County Agent and that means ride around in a Ford, and that in turn means women and they mean excitement, and that's Felk. His rate of speed between Henniker and Laconia is two days under normal conditions. His rate of speed when a suicide is at stake has never been computed. He's a life saver. At present he is trying to "be steady in Durham," but it's hard; oh, so hard.
text:
Nobody wants to cultivate me, I'm wild
"Felk's" specialties are Aggie courses, Fords, and women. The first is simple, but it gets him the job of Assistant County Agent and that means ride around in a Ford, and that in turn means women and they mean excitement, and that's Felk. His rate of speed between Henniker and Laconia is two days under normal conditions. His rate of speed when a suicide is at stake has never been computed. He's a life saver. At present he is trying to "be steady in Durham," but it's hard; oh, so hard.

name: Fitch, Harold Wakefield
motto: He paints with iodine all his pains, And often gets seasick when it rains, Had the war continued for many years Would he have been an admiral or where he is?
description: "Anyone can join the army," but just see what they take in the navy! Some men are far-sighted—perhaps "Fitchy" was in this case, for sailors seldom go beyond "England." The good ship "Morrill Hall" was an unstable old craft, but it's doubtful if she ever rolled more than 45 (degrees). Once he entered a turkey race and came out with a rooster, but cheer up—someone had to be last!
text:
He paints with iodine all his pains, And often gets seasick when it rains, Had the war continued for many years Would he have been an admiral or where he is?
"Anyone can join the army," but just see what they take in the navy! Some men are far-sighted—perhaps "Fitchy" was in this case, for sailors seldom go beyond "England." The good ship "Morrill Hall" was an unstable old craft, but it's doubtful if she ever rolled more than 45 (degrees). Once he entered a turkey race and came out with a rooster, but cheer up—someone had to be last!

name: Fitts, Perley Irving
motto: His legs bow out and his feet toe in, But he's a pretty good man for the shape he's in.
clubs: Assistant Business Manager of the 1920 GRANITE
description: One day as Perley was looking over his Swamproot Almanac, he found that there was such a place as a college, where men went to assimilate greater knowledge. He had been to the Grange, once or twice, and heard people talk about it, but still he was not sure whether "college" was some sort of a county fair or just another form of a circus. He wanted a "Normal Course" like what they have in Keene, but finally decided to be a "sod-buster." The first two years found Perley burning the midnight oil, but now that he is an A Z, he drifts along on his reputation.
text:
His legs bow out and his feet toe in, But he's a pretty good man for the shape he's in.
One day as Perley was looking over his Swamproot Almanac, he found that there was such a place as a college, where men went to assimilate greater knowledge. He wanted a "Normal Course" like what they have in Keene, but finally decided to be a "sod-buster." The first two years found Perley burning the midnight oil, but now that he is an A Z, he drifts along on his reputation.

name: Foster, Russel Chase
motto: He hath a mint of formulae in his mind
description: If an analysis were taken of Pecker's brain, one would find integral signs and moments of Inertia, with a slight trace of Lee Hill. Pecker's curiosity has caused his lab. instructors a considerable amount of worry. As for taking things apart, maybe his army life insurance will come in handy some day. Even though his interest for social activities is equivalent to the derivative of a constant, we hope that with the aid of the Engineering Society dances (?) Pecker will be able to fox-trot before the Junior Prom.
text:
He hath a mint of formulae in his mind
If an analysis were taken of Pecker's brain, one would find integral signs and moments of Inertia, with a slight trace of Lee Hill. Pecker's curiosity has caused his lab. instructors a considerable amount of worry. As for taking things apart, maybe his army life insurance will come in handy some day. Even though his interest for social activities is equivalent to the derivative of a constant, we hope that with the aid of the Engineering Society dances (?) Pecker will be able to fox-trot before the Junior Prom.

name: Furber, Miriam Louise
motto: Her words do show her wit incomparable
description: Miriam with her ready wit and quizzical smile, has already proved to us that what was Wheaton's loss was New Hampshire's gain. She is interested in all economic and political questions of the day, especially in Home Rule; and she seems to side with the "Irish." "Miri-ham," tell us why.
text:
Her words do show her wit incomparable
Miriam with her ready wit and quizzical smile, has already proved to us that what was Wheaton's loss was New Hampshire's gain. She is interested in all economic and political questions of the day, especially in Home Rule; and she seems to side with the "Irish." "Miri-ham," tell us why.

name: Gardner, Celia Hubbard
motto: O, Melancholy, turn thine eyes away!
description: Celia used to say, "What could be sweeter than those days at COLBY Academy?" Last summer she decided that ambulance driving in France would go one better; and just as she passed her training course with high honor, the armistice was signed. Once again she is back in Durham, helping her friends to chase away the "blues." Her disappointment at not going overseas ran high; but as she philosophically expresses it, "It's all a part of the great plan."
text:
O, Melancholy, turn thine eyes away!
Celia used to say, "What could be sweeter than those days at COLBY Academy?" Last summer she decided that ambulance driving in France would go one better; and just as she passed her training course with high honor, the armistice was signed. Once again she is back in Durham, helping her friends to chase away the "blues." Her disappointment at not going overseas ran high; but as she philosophically expresses it, "It's all a part of the great plan."

name: Greer, Raymond Chase
description: Bud one night had a telephone call, We'll all agree t'was no offense, But we can find no reason at all, Why he left his pants on the barbed wire fence. Bud dropped in from Grasmere—of which Manchester is a suburb. His good looks are exceeded only by his social activities. From authentic sources we learn that he has started several young ladies on the road to matrimony, but is still a bachelor.
text: Bud one night had a telephone call, We'll all agree t'was no offense, But we can find no reason at all, Why he left his pants on the barbed wire fence. Bud dropped in from Grasmere—of which Manchester is a suburb. His good looks are exceeded only by his social activities. From authentic sources we learn that he has started several young ladies on the road to matrimony, but is still a bachelor.

name: Gove, Norris Dickinson
description: Norris came to us from Sanborn Seminary and proceeded to be one of the favored few to survive "Gary's" Math. courses. As a student he is a faithful old grind, but still he enjoys his "playtime" too. As a Cross Country man, "Dinty" learned to like cold baths and has even been seen journeying down to Pettee creek and bathing in its snow-flecked waters.
text: Norris came to us from Sanborn Seminary and proceeded to be one of the favored few to survive "Gary's" Math. courses. As a student he is a faithful old grind, but still he enjoys his "playtime" too. As a Cross Country man, "Dinty" learned to like cold baths and has even been seen journeying down to Pettee creek and bathing in its snow-flecked waters.

name: Ham, Harold Rudman
motto: In the class of '20 there is a Ham, In spite of that he's quite a man; He likes to draw on his old briar pipe And grow three hairs on his upper lip
description: "Hammie" is a practical man. There is no danger that he will ever be taken for a minister or a Professor of English, but if he doesn't look out he will be a horse doctor. For some time he has been trying to decide whether he had better buy a razor or wait until next Christmas and see what Santa Claus has to offer.
text:
In the class of '20 there is a Ham, In spite of that he's quite a man; He likes to draw on his old briar pipe And grow three hairs on his upper lip
"Hammie" is a practical man. There is no danger that he will ever be taken for a minister or a Professor of English, but if he doesn't look out he will be a horse doctor. For some time he has been trying to decide whether he had better buy a razor or wait until next Christmas and see what Santa Claus has to offer.

name: Harling, Harry Jonathan
motto: He whose own worth doth speak, need not speak his own worth
description: "Husky," tall and strong, rolled into Durham from away down in East Jaffrey in his little flivver. He has since been learning to tell the East Jaffrey farmers "how to do it" and in his spare time he rushes the Durham hills in "Henry," practicing for cross country by trotting to the nearest town for new tires. We hope to visit Husky's model farm in years to come and we sure expect some farm, for any man that pulls 100% in an A. H. course must spend some time on his subject. If you want to know the NAME of any Co-ed, ask Harl.

If you want to know the NAME of any Co-ed, ask Harl.

name: Hayes, Ethelle Meserve
motto: Many days shall see her, And yet no day without A deed to crown it
description: She is gentle, but firm. Her sweet disposition and kindly interest for everybody is unfailing. Really, though, is there any reason why Ethelle is specializing in Household Economics when she is such a shark in Mathematics?
text:
Many days shall see her, And yet no day without A deed to crown it
She is gentle, but firm. Her sweet disposition and kindly interest for everybody is unfailing. Really, though, is there any reason why Ethelle is specializing in Household Economics when she is such a shark in Mathematics?

name: Herlihy, Abby Katherine
motto: A hard worker and a jolly good fellow withal
clubs: Assistant Business Manager The New Hampshire
description: Although Abby makes very little noise we are always sure to find her "there," whether it be at social life or studies. When she was a Freshman, it is said she kept very EARLY hours studying mathematics; but now it is rumored that she makes chemistry her specialty. We wonder why? We are sure she will be successful in any line of work she undertakes; whether it be chemistry or gymnastics.
text:
A hard worker and a jolly good fellow withal
Although Abby makes very little noise we are always sure to find her "there," whether it be at social life or studies. When she was a Freshman, it is said she kept very EARLY hours studying mathematics; but now it is rumored that she makes chemistry her specialty. We wonder why? We are sure she will be successful in any line of work she undertakes; whether it be chemistry or gymnastics.

name: Hill, Benjamin Franklin
motto: Ben's new breeches were wrecked one day, For with Sulphuric Acid he was bound to play; It ate his legs clean thru to the marrow And he had to come home concealed in a Barrel.
description: Ben is a connoisseur in tango twirl, fitting with the fair sex like a duck's foot in the mud. During the summer months Ben was busily occupied showing the draftees how to do Squads East and getting them in practice so they could salute him when he came back with his leather puttees.
text:
Ben's new breeches were wrecked one day, For with Sulphuric Acid he was bound to play; It ate his legs clean thru to the marrow And he had to come home concealed in a Barrel.
Ben is a connoisseur in tango twirl, fitting with the fair sex like a duck's foot in the mud. During the summer months Ben was busily occupied showing the draftees how to do Squads East and getting them in practice so they could salute him when he came back with his leather puttees.

name: Hill, William Rodney
motto: Oh, what may man within him hide Though angel on the outer side
description: His devilish eyes and pink cheeks won for him the heart of a fair Co-ed and now its "Portland forever!" or "why Ducker goes broke every week-end." As a chauffeur, Rodney sure can peddle milk. When asked why he came to New Hampshire College, Rodney blushed and glanced passionately at Smith Hall. Still we must admit few have attained the popularity of our good-natured Rodney.

His devilish eyes and pink cheeks won for him the heart of a fair Co-ed and now its "Portland forever!" or "why Ducker goes broke every week-end." As a chauffeur, Rodney sure can peddle milk. When asked why he came to New Hampshire College, Rodney blushed and glanced passionately at Smith Hall. Still we must admit few have attained the popularity of our good-natured Rodney.

name: Huse, Walter Daniel
description: Walter came to Durham under the watchful eye and tender care of his big sister and for the most part of his Freshman existence behaved very well. Gradually, however, sister lost control and Dame Rumor attached such misdemeanors to Walt's college career as midnight auto parties, occasional dips into the mysteries of "Cocheco Street," and even the missing link between Pa Curtis and "?".
text: Walter came to Durham under the watchful eye and tender care of his big sister and for the most part of his Freshman existence behaved very well. Gradually, however, sister lost control and Dame Rumor attached such misdemeanors to Walt's college career as midnight auto parties, occasional dips into the mysteries of "Cocheco Street," and even the missing link between Pa Curtis and "?".

name: Jenness, Judith Varney
motto: "A maiden never bold, Of spirit so still and quiet, that her motion Blushed at herself."
description: Here, indeed, is a typically sweet and demure Quaker maiden. Many are the thanks due to her for her untiring efforts in behalf of The New Hampshire. If you would like to see a real blush, ask Judith why she isn't a vegetarian.
text:
"A maiden never bold, Of spirit so still and quiet, that her motion Blushed at herself."
Here, indeed, is a typically sweet and demure Quaker maiden. Many are the thanks due to her for her untiring efforts in behalf of The New Hampshire. If you would like to see a real blush, ask Judith why she isn't a vegetarian.

name: Hunt, Esther Hazel
motto: "The mildest manners and the gentlest heart"
description: Esther came all the way from Oregon with her wagon hitched to a star—she was going to join the professional class as an English teacher. Among the vocational men staying at Durham this summer, "Along came Steve." For some unknown reason the idea of teaching English suddenly lost its charm and the following semester, she registered in the Home Economics Course. But Esther is happy in her northern home, for often we hear her singing, "There's a Long, Long Trail," and "When Steve Comes Marching Home Again."
text:
"The mildest manners and the gentlest heart"
Esther came all the way from Oregon with her wagon hitched to a star—she was going to join the professional class as an English teacher. Among the vocational men staying at Durham this summer, "Along came Steve." For some unknown reason the idea of teaching English suddenly lost its charm and the following semester, she registered in the Home Economics Course. But Esther is happy in her northern home, for often we hear her singing, "There's a Long, Long Trail," and "When Steve Comes Marching Home Again."

name: Jenness, Leslie George
motto: The longest way 'round is the shortest way home
description: "Les" believes in this motto else why does he go home via Boston (?) That's another question, we think. "Jen" came to Durham a Simon-pure woman-hater, but his fancies soon changed. 'Tis said, too, that this jolly chap of ours has succeeded in sharking the "King's" courses. He has made successful analysis of the elements of chemical compounds, but the analysis of the unknown at Smith Hall is still a "Question."
text:
The longest way 'round is the shortest way home
"Les" believes in this motto else why does he go home via Boston (?) That's another question, we think. "Jen" came to Durham a Simon-pure woman-hater, but his fancies soon changed. 'Tis said, too, that this jolly chap of ours has succeeded in sharking the "King's" courses. He has made successful analysis of the elements of chemical compounds, but the analysis of the unknown at Smith Hall is still a "Question."

name: Jones, Elizabeth
motto: Candor, rarest of flowers
description: Since Elizabeth came here from Middlebury she has won our esteem by her sincere manner and her frank opinions. "Lib" has distinctive ideas about things and people, college customs in particular, and she never hesitates to give them when they are asked for. Her favorite occupation is sleeping, although here at college she has to do without the comfort of her usual number of cats. We think that she would have made a good grenade thrower if her skill in heaving biscuits is any indication.
text:
Candor, rarest of flowers
Since Elizabeth came here from Middlebury she has won our esteem by her sincere manner and her frank opinions. "Lib" has distinctive ideas about things and people, college customs in particular, and she never hesitates to give them when they are asked for. Her favorite occupation is sleeping, although here at college she has to do without the comfort of her usual number of cats. We think that she would have made a good grenade thrower if her skill in heaving biscuits is any indication.

name: Jones, Lucie Jeanette
motto: A Friend for Friendship's Sake
description: During her college career Lucie has qualified as a singer, a poetess and all around good sport. Her activities have been varied. She finds time to familiarize herself with the "ins and outs" of all the girls' dorms, the Granite State Nurseries and Dean Pettee's water tower. She has learned that they have mud in Lee, police in Exeter and that there is a beach at Rye. We are wondering how she is able to take part in these various activities and still "fit" with the Profs. and the girls' dorm matrons.
text:
A Friend for Friendship's Sake
During her college career Lucie has qualified as a singer, a poetess and all around good sport. Her activities have been varied. She finds time to familiarize herself with the "ins and outs" of all the girls' dorms, the Granite State Nurseries and Dean Pettee's water tower. She has learned that they have mud in Lee, police in Exeter and that there is a beach at Rye. We are wondering how she is able to take part in these various activities and still "fit" with the Profs. and the girls' dorm matrons.

name: Grace, Mae Joy
motto: Let us, then, be what we are and speak what we think
description: Grace is one of those girls who goes her own sweet way, regardless of the world. Her interest in scholarship lines is centered in the department of Education and Psychology. Her favorite pastime is that of frequenting the dance halls in surrounding towns. In spite of her breezy and self-sufficient disposition, nevertheless, it must be admitted that Grace knows her "Place."
text:
Let us, then, be what we are and speak what we think
Grace is one of those girls who goes her own sweet way, regardless of the world. Her interest in scholarship lines is centered in the department of Education and Psychology. Her favorite pastime is that of frequenting the dance halls in surrounding towns. In spite of her breezy and self-sufficient disposition, nevertheless, it must be admitted that Grace knows her "Place."

name: Kelley, Florence Aura
motto: To be merry, best becomes you
description: Florence's coiffure is as wonderful as her sunny disposition and her smile. Her strong preference of "Dawn" to darkness is in accordance with this characteristic. Florence is "game" for everything that is going, but her pet hobby is dancing. In spite of the war, she is as loyal as ever to her German.
text:
To be merry, best becomes you
Florence's coiffure is as wonderful as her sunny disposition and her smile. Her strong preference of "Dawn" to darkness is in accordance with this characteristic. Florence is "game" for everything that is going, but her pet hobby is dancing. In spite of the war, she is as loyal as ever to her German.

name: Kling, Frances
motto: Full pleasant 'tis to see one's name in print
description: We did not know "Franny" very well her Freshman year until we learned her skill in reading characters from handwriting. That, together with her other hobby, typing, has entertained many of us. She is surely a "Soci shark" and languages follow closely at its heels. We all wonder where these abilities will finally lead her.
text:
Full pleasant 'tis to see one's name in print
We did not know "Franny" very well her Freshman year until we learned her skill in reading characters from handwriting. That, together with her other hobby, typing, has entertained many of us. She is surely a "Soci shark" and languages follow closely at its heels. We all wonder where these abilities will finally lead her.

name: Ladd, Frank Watson
motto: All mankind loves a lover
description: Coming to New Hampshire with the intention of completing an engineering course in four years, he recently discovered that "all work and no play makes Jack a dull boy" and now plans to win his B.S. in five years, though we believe he deserves it now. King of the fox trotters, Caruso the second (I hear you calling me), and Paderewski's one-piece orchestra are but a few of Frank's many accomplishments. He is a firm believer in Lydia Pinkham's pink pills for pale people.
text:
All mankind loves a lover
Coming to New Hampshire with the intention of completing an engineering course in four years, he recently discovered that "all work and no play makes Jack a dull boy" and now plans to win his B.S. in five years, though we believe he deserves it now. King of the fox trotters, Caruso the second (I hear you calling me), and Paderewski's one-piece orchestra are but a few of Frank's many accomplishments. He is a firm believer in Lydia Pinkham's pink pills for pale people.

name: Ladd, Harold Marden
motto: Hi diddle, diddle, Dad Ladd and his fiddle, He never will be the same, for once on the deck He got hit in the neck, and didn't know who was to blame
description: Whether Dad learned to pick strawberries at New Hampton Literary and Biblical Institute or not, is still a matter of conjecture. Father sincerely loves his Alma Mater, but there are rumors that he will soon transfer to Williams. He is a docile creature and a well-trained parlor athlete, responding readily to "My Dear Father." He may be seen meandering over the campus with a book bag in his left hand, measuring the distance between classes by that characteristic, even, musing stride.

Hi diddle, diddle, Dad Ladd and his fiddle, He never will be the same, for once on the deck He got hit in the neck, and didn't know who was to blame
Whether Dad learned to pick strawberries at New Hampton Literary and Biblical Institute or not, is still a matter of conjecture. He may be seen meandering over the campus with a book bag in his left hand, measuring the distance between classes by that characteristic, even, musing stride.

name: Lane, Chester Linwood
motto: Chet hails from Concord, the 'Capital' city. His college record reads like the medal list of an Irish General. Like the Alchemist of old, he tries to substitute the baser for the purer metals. He has, however, failed to substitute a 'Lane' for a 'King.' A smile from a pretty girl is to 'Chet' like water to a thirsty camel. 'Chet' seems to have mastered the chemical dictionary and delights in using it to awe his hearers.
text: Chet hails from Concord, the 'Capital' city. His college record reads like the medal list of an Irish General. Like the Alchemist of old, he tries to substitute the baser for the purer metals. He has, however, failed to substitute a 'Lane' for a 'King.' A smile from a pretty girl is to 'Chet' like water to a thirsty camel. 'Chet' seems to have mastered the chemical dictionary and delights in using it to awe his hearers.

name: Langley, Mildred Mae
motto: The best things usually come in small packages
description: "Mimi" has an advantage over most of us, and that is, she lives at home. If any one inquires who that "Little" girl is around the campus, you may be sure it is "Mimi." Although small, she is very capable, and when there is anything to be done, she is right there! Mildred, why do you go to the post office after each Boston mail comes in?
text:
The best things usually come in small packages
"Mimi" has an advantage over most of us, and that is, she lives at home. If any one inquires who that "Little" girl is around the campus, you may be sure it is "Mimi." Although small, she is very capable, and when there is anything to be done, she is right there! Mildred, why do you go to the post office after each Boston mail comes in?

name: Leavitt, Harold Irving
motto: Smile, gol darn you, smile
description: Smiles started in as an Aggie student, but became ambitious and changed over into the engineering course. While playing football against Tufts last year Smiles took a short course in astronomy and claims that this course has the Dean's course beaten a mile. Smiles thought he was sharking a course in Smith Hall, 52, but flunked out cold in the finals with no chance for a make up. Now he devotes all his time to studies (?) and telling how the "City by the Smell" is "clear agates."
text:

Now he devotes all his time to studies (?) and telling how the "City by the Smell" is "clear agates."

name: Lewis, Miriam
motto: Warm hearted, sparkling with fun, She's sure to win you before she's done
description: When a Freshman Miriam was believed by some people to be a very quiet girl, who didn't socialize very much. Those people are now punching themselves to see if they are awake. Earnest, enthusiastic and sincere, Miriam is right there always ready for anything, from a feed to an all night discussion on sociological problems. If you don't believe it, just try her!
text:
Warm hearted, sparkling with fun, She's sure to win you before she's done
When a Freshman Miriam was believed by some people to be a very quiet girl, who didn't socialize very much. Those people are now punching themselves to see if they are awake. Earnest, enthusiastic and sincere, Miriam is right there always ready for anything, from a feed to an all night discussion on sociological problems. If you don't believe it, just try her!

name: Litch, Richard Corning
motto: One, two, three, four
description: "Ben" chased accommodation trains for two years and liked the job, but "Garry" decided that if he didn't care enough about Math to get here at 8:00 o'clock, he could make up the time another year. When it comes to "Bull throwing" Ben can't be beat. His subjects are many and his gas is unlimited. We wonder if he gets a commission from the Stanley Company on every steam car sold. He is a real engineer and when his work is done he spends his time commuting with the "Knight." He sharked the course at Plattsburg this summer, specializing in K. P., due to calling some of the non-coms pet names.
text:
One, two, three, four
"Ben" chased accommodation trains for two years and liked the job, but "Garry" decided that if he didn't care enough about Math to get here at 8:00 o'clock, he could make up the time another year. He is a real engineer and when his work is done he spends his time commuting with the "Knight." He sharked the course at Plattsburg this summer, specializing in K. P., due to calling some of the non-coms pet names.

name: McQuesten, Ruth Carolyn
motto: I'm here, I'm there, I'm everywhere. I dance, I sing, I break men's hearts
clubs: Secretary N. H. College Auxiliary Red Cross (2)
description: 'Tis she, indeed, for as sympathetic observers, Ruth's friends find it difficult to keep pace with her latest notion for which she is especially adapted to become in the future. Ruthie is a vowed "why worry-er," but she is not true to her faith, for she possesses one besetting problem, and all her conscience comes from contemplating: "If I go with Jen, what 'would-he' do?"
text:
I'm here, I'm there, I'm everywhere. I dance, I sing, I break men's hearts
'Tis she, indeed, for as sympathetic observers, Ruth's friends find it difficult to keep pace with her latest notion for which she is especially adapted to become in the future. Ruthie is a vowed "why worry-er," but she is not true to her faith, for she possesses one besetting problem, and all her conscience comes from contemplating: "If I go with Jen, what 'would-he' do?"

name: Melville, George Donald
motto: Old friends were better than new
description: While occupying a front seat in the opera house at Newmarket 'tis said that "Don" fell in love with one of the leading characters in "Uncle Tom's Cabin." "Don" aspires to dancing but is cut out for a cross country runner. One steps, waltzes, fox trots and cross country races are all done in the same time by Don. But cheer up, "Don," all's fair in love and war.
text:
Old friends were better than new
While occupying a front seat in the opera house at Newmarket 'tis said that "Don" fell in love with one of the leading characters in "Uncle Tom's Cabin." "Don" aspires to dancing but is cut out for a cross country runner. One steps, waltzes, fox trots and cross country races are all done in the same time by Don. But cheer up, "Don," all's fair in love and war.

name: Meras, Norman E.
clubs: First Lieutenant Co. D, R. O. T. C. (3)
description: For three years Meras has been trying to absorb all the knowledge the Engineering Department can give, and it is safe to say he would succeed except for one thing, and that is he has difficulty in studying more than twenty-four hours a day. As an agent of "Stanley Stevens" he has only one rival ("Ben" Litch). 'Tis said that in his Freshman year he did everything up "Brown."
text: For three years Meras has been trying to absorb all the knowledge the Engineering Department can give, and it is safe to say he would succeed except for one thing, and that is he has difficulty in studying more than twenty-four hours a day. As an agent of "Stanley Stevens" he has only one rival ("Ben" Litch). 'Tis said that in his Freshman year he did everything up "Brown."

name: Meserve, Jessica Frances
motto: It's good to be merry and wise, It's good to be honest and true
description: Jessica came here as a Domestic Science student and her excellence in the art of housekeeping is shown very clearly in the Rest Room. Jessica is very learned in the art of tumbling down gracefully, for she always gets the first recorded fall of the season. One word of advice, Jessica, when you have cinders in your eyes, be careful whom you try to embrace. His wife might be near.
text:
It's good to be merry and wise, It's good to be honest and true
Jessica came here as a Domestic Science student and her excellence in the art of housekeeping is shown very clearly in the Rest Room. Jessica is very learned in the art of tumbling down gracefully, for she always gets the first recorded fall of the season. One word of advice, Jessica, when you have cinders in your eyes, be careful whom you try to embrace. His wife might be near.

name: Mitchell, Basil Joseph
motto: As idle as a painted ship Upon a painted ocean
description: During his Freshman year "Mitch" left his girl in Nashua long enough to attend a few classes. He broke his ankle chasing the co-eds his Soph year, so spent a few weeks flirting with the nurses in Dover. Mitch is always kicking about the Durham water; it is whispered he goes strong for Poland Spring. Mitch saw much service but little water, as he was in the U. S. N. R. F. stationed at Bumkin Island. Mitch says it was a tough war.

Mitch says it was a tough war.

name: Morrill, Clyde
description: This young man is another one of our quiet commuters. He was quite prominent with the gang in the club room, being a charter member of the Parson's Pitch Club, but with the closing of the Club Room this boy fast developed into a third degree library lizard. Clyde is a handy man to have around, though, since he always has a goodly supply of Lucky Strikes on hand.

He was quite prominent with the gang in the club room, being a charter member of the Parson's Pitch Club, but with the closing of the Club Room this boy fast developed into a third degree library lizard. name: Morrison, Cecil Alister
motto: "Fat but cute, yet nobody loves a fat man (?)"
description: Laugh and be fat. Cecil laughed the "King" into believing that he was built for a "chemiker," but "Olie" called his bluff. Though built more for comfort than for speed, he drove to victory with Morrison's steed. Cease got his incentive for high finance by raffling stale chocolates and BBB (?) pipes in the dorm, but remember, Cecil, Napoleon was a little man.
text:
"Fat but cute, yet nobody loves a fat man (?)"
Laugh and be fat. Cecil laughed the "King" into believing that he was built for a "chemiker," but "Olie" called his bluff. Though built more for comfort than for speed, he drove to victory with Morrison's steed. Cease got his incentive for high finance by raffling stale chocolates and BBB (?) pipes in the dorm, but remember, Cecil, Napoleon was a little man.

name: Murphy, Helen Ann
motto: "It is as though you had touched a loyal hand, looked into brown eyes, and made a noble friend"
description: When Helen comes home from the "lab" bearing a knowledge, she decided to be a chemist and nothing that "Arnie" or "The King" said, could dissuade her, so she registered as a "chemical engineer," thereby establishing a precedent. When Helen comes home from the "lab" bearing a box of Lowney's won on a bet from some fellow-chemist, it sets the other co-eds pondering over the fact that there are other things in the chem course than formulae and chemical reactions. A true friend, always pleasant and cheerful, fortune can do nothing too good for her. Here's to her success.
